title: Fix PDF highlight download duplicates and flat mode
description:
PipeSync "Download plans" highlight export showed duplicate strokes in Edge, Okular, and Adobe for both Baked in Highlights and Editable Highlights. Baked mode also stayed editable in Adobe and round caps looked wrong.

Root cause: flat export still wrote /Ink annotations (InkList + BS + /LE Round). Viewers render those alongside any prior baked ink when clear missed an annot, and /LE Round on Ink produces broken cap geometry. Flat Ink annots are also editable in Adobe by design.

Fix (dbg38): Baked in Highlights now draws each line once into the page content stream (pdf-lib drawLine, round caps) after stripping all Ink/Highlight annots — truly baked in, not editable. Editable Highlights keeps a single InkList + BS annot per line with no /AP or /LE; annot clear is more aggressive (InkList/QuadPoints fallback, delete empty /Annots, up to 4 passes).

Hard-refresh PipeSync after deploy (Ctrl+Shift+R). Check console for [hp-download-debug] — inkAfterClear should be 0 before burn; flatContentStream true for baked mode.

Who is affected: Private OVH server. SaaS unchanged until you publish a platform release.
changeLog: Baked in Highlights: content-stream strokes (round caps, not editable in Adobe).
Editable Highlights: one InkList + BS annot per line; no /AP or /LE double layers.
Stronger pre-burn annot clear (InkList detect, empty /Annots delete, retry loop).
Cache bust dbg38 for app.js, pipesync-boot.js, and pdf-highlight-shape.
